data.title: Market rallies with Tesla and Robinhood leading, but Snowflake falls on earnings outlook. Dow Jones Futures impacted.
data.description: On Wednesday, the market experienced a significant rally with a strong performance from Tesla and Robinhood. However, Snowflake saw a decline following its earnings projection. The Dow Jones Futures reported that Snowflake's outlook led to a decrease after a widespread surge driven by Tesla and Robinhood. This news highlights the fluctuations in the market, with notable movements in key companies.

data.title: UMC collaborates with Polar Semiconductor in the US for 8-inch wafer production, focusing on key industries.
data.description: United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) recently announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Polar Semiconductor to potentially collaborate on scalable 8-inch wafer production in the United States. The partnership would focus on U.S.-based scalable 8-inch wafer manufacturing, with the intention of providing high-quality chips essential to various industries such as automotive, data centers, consumer electronics, aerospace, and defense. According to the MOU, selected devices will be manufactured at Polar Semiconductor's expanded 8-inch facility in Minnesota, utilizing UMC's technology expertise and extensive global customer network. The collaboration aims to drive growth, assist in customers' multi-sourcing strategies, and enhance the U.S. 8-inch manufacturing capabilities.

data.title: 400+ global AI and tech leaders gathered at the Tech Basel Miami AI Summit focused on cross-sector transformation.
data.description: On December 3rd, 2025, the Tech Basel Miami AI Summit took place in Miami, attracting over 400 participants. The event was carefully organized to drive innovation in artificial intelligence, finance, healthcare, and national security sectors. Held at the Mayfair, the summit aimed to foster collaboration and spark positive changes across industries.

data.title: Purple Biotech to showcase CAPTN-3 platform at ESMO-IO 2025 Congress, presenting new preclinical data on its anti-tumor activity. Event in London from Dec 10-12.
data.description: Purple Biotech Ltd., a clinical-stage company traded on NASDAQ/TASE as PPBT, unveiled groundbreaking data highlighting the efficacy of its CAPTN-3 platform in combating treatment-resistant cancers. The unique multi-arm design of the platform demonstrated remarkable anti-tumor activity, showcasing its versatility in addressing challenging cases. The Company is set to present these new preclinical findings at the European Society of Medical Oncology Immuno-Oncology 2025 Annual Congress in London, United Kingdom, from December 10–12, 2025.

data.title: BD and ChemoGLO™ partner to expand hazardous drug contamination testing for facilities and labs. Dec. 4, 2025. (NYSE: BDX)
data.description: On December 4, 2025, in Franklin Lakes, New Jersey, BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company), a prominent worldwide medical technology enterprise listed on the NYSE with the ticker symbol BDX, teamed up with ChemoGLO™, a renowned expert in detecting and decontaminating hazardous drugs. This collaboration has been established with the aim of progressing initiatives related to hazardous drug safety.

data.title: Dollar General's Q3 2025 results: Net sales $10.6B, Same-Store Sales up 2.5%, Operating Profit $425.9M, EPS $1.28. Dividend $0.
data.description: In Goodlettsville, Tennessee, Dollar General Corporation (NYSE: DG) released its financial performance for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025, covering a 13-week period ending on October 31, 2025. The company reported a 4.6% increase in net sales, totaling $10.6 billion, with same-store sales also rising by 2.5%. Operating profit saw a significant boost of 31.5% to $425.9 million, while diluted earnings per share (“EPS”) surged by 43.8% to $1.28. Year-to-date cash flows from operations were up by 28.4% to $2.8 billion. Furthermore, the Board of Directors announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.

data.title: US suspends sanctions on China, halts export controls, aims to maintain trade truce. Focus now on reducing reliance on China for rare earths.
data.description: The United States has decided to halt the imposition of sanctions on China's Ministry of State Security in order to uphold the trade truce between the two countries. This decision comes in light of a significant cyberespionage campaign, known as Salt Typhoon. The Trump administration has also chosen not to implement major new export controls against China. Sources indicate a shift in the administration's China strategy towards maintaining stability until the U.S. can lessen its dependency on China for rare earths. This reliance has restricted Washington's capability to enact more stringent measures. Trump's focus is on decreasing this reliance while upholding the trade truce.

data.title: EYA appoints Robin Bettarel as Head of Multifamily to lead growth with 2,000+ rental units in DMV.
data.description: EYA LLC, a prominent urban infill residential developer in Washington, D.C., has appointed Robin Bettarel as the new Head of Multifamily. This decision comes as part of EYA's recent focus on expanding its urban townhome business to include a significant market-rate and mixed-income multifamily rental portfolio. Their multifamily pipeline in the DMV region now exceeds 2,000 units. Bettarel's hiring is a crucial move in advancing this growth strategy and enhancing EYA's presence in the market.

data.title: Earnings report for December 4, 2025:- BRP: $0.88/share, $1.46B revenue- BETA: -$0.44/share, $6.71M revenue- Donaldson: $0.93/share, $923.28M revenue- BMO: $2.15/share, $6.40B revenue- CM: $1.84/share, $6.43B revenue- CRMT: -$0.57/share, $331.02M revenue- TIGR: $0.21/share, $128.15M revenue- TD: $1.46/share, $11.05B revenue- SAIC: $2.12/share, $1.86B revenue- Kroger: $1.04/share, $34.22B revenue
data.description: Several companies are set to report their quarterly earnings before the bell. Some of the key numbers include BRP (NASDAQ: DOOO) expected to report earnings of $0.88 per share on revenue of $1.46 billion. BETA Technologies (NYSE: BETA) is likely to show a quarterly loss of $0.44 per share on revenue of $6.71 million. Donaldson (NYSE: DCI) is estimated to report earnings of $0.93 per share on revenue of $923.28 million. Bank of Montreal (NYSE: BMO) is projected to report earnings of $2.15 per share on revenue of $6.40 billion, while Canadian Imperial Bank (NYSE: CM) is expected to report earnings of $1.84 per share on revenue of $6.43 billion. America's Car-Mart (NASDAQ: CRMT) may report a quarterly loss of $0.57 per share on revenue of $331.02 million, and UP Fintech Holding (NASDAQ: TIGR) is projected to report earnings of $0.21 per share on revenue of $128.15 million. Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE: TD) is estimated to report earnings of $1.46 per share on revenue of $11.05 billion, and Science Applications Intl (NASDAQ: SAIC) is projected to report earnings of $2.12 per share on revenue of $1.86 billion. Kroger (NYSE: KR) is expected to show earnings of $1.04 per share on revenue of $34.22 billion. The article can be found on Benzinga.com for further details.

data.title: Build-A-Bear Workshop's Q3 2025 revenue hits a record $122.7M, with $375.3M for first nine months. Pre-tax income was $10.7M.
data.description: In St. Louis, Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (NYSE: BBW) released its third-quarter fiscal year 2025 results, concluding on November 1, 2025. The quarter saw total revenues reach a record-breaking $122.7 million, showing growth from $119.4 million. Over the first nine months of the year, total revenues also marked a record of $375.3 million, up from $346.0 million. Pre-tax income for the quarter was $10.7 million compared to $13.1 million, impacted by approximately $4 million due to tariffs and related costs.

data.title: Crescent Biopharma partners with Kelun-Biotech, secures $185M funding, accelerates Next Gen Therapeutics for Solid Tumors.
data.description: Collaborating with Kelun-Biotech allows for the expansion of the pipeline and speeds up the combination approach with CR-001, a bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, along with several antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). This partnership aims to produce clinical data for these products in various international markets as well as in Greater China.

data.title: Middleby will sell 51% of its Residential Kitchen business to 26North for $885 million, retaining 49% stake.
data.description: Elgin, Illinois - The Middleby Corporation (NASDAQ: MIDD) revealed a new development where it will be selling a 51% share in its Residential Kitchen division to entities affiliated with 26North Partners LP. This deal, valued at $885 million, entails the creation of a standalone joint venture for the Residential Kitchen segment. Middleby will retain a 49% interest as a non-controlling partner in this venture and will receive an upfront cash payment.
